title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, and Production Defense · Execution Trace: Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback
summary: An undocumented API changes nullable fields, serves stale generated docs, deploys a new binary before migrations, drains no traffic, and cannot tie an incident response to source or schema. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo deploy migrate drain rollback increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build once, verify artifact and config, expand storage safely, stage readiness, canary, observe, drain with Shutdown, contract storage, and rollback by invariant.
Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, status, errors, ordering, auth, caching, and behavior; then plan deprecation and migration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback · Theory Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4340 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate one OpenAPI-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and rollback release; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback contract for the supplied data.
